attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so m…
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep…
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/…
- claude-api: Build Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); or asks to add a Claude feature (prompt caching, ad…
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- cma-doctor: Validate Concept Model Kit installation
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- cma-normalize: Apply whitespace normalization to concept model documents
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-validate: Validate concept model links and annotations
- cma-render: Transform concept model to ClaudeMark format
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution

content.text:
Claude Opus 4.7 was released just 2 days ago (April 16, 2026). You're currently running **Opus 4.6** (`claude-opus-4-6[1m]`).

The reason you don't see it yet is likely that Claude Code hasn't rolled out the Opus 4.7 model option. It typically takes some time after a model launch for it to propagate to all surfaces — the API, Claude.ai, and Claude Code may update on different schedules. You could check for a Claude Code update (`claude update`) or watch the changelog.

Sources:
- [Claude Opus 4.7 is generally available - GitHub Changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-04-16-claude-opus-4-7-is-generally-available/)
- [What's new in Claude Opus 4.7 - Claude API Docs](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/whats-new-claude-4-7)
- [Anthropic rolls out Claude Opus 4.7 - CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/anthropic-claude-opus-4-7-model-mythos.html)
- [Release notes - Claude Help Center](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12138966-release-notes)
- [Claude Opus 4.7 on Amazon Bedrock - AWS](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/introducing-anthropics-claude-opus-4-7-model-in-amazon-bedrock/)

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Opus 4.7 and Claude Code CLI availability in 2026:

## Claude Opus 4.7 Release

Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7 on April 16, 2026, a meaningful upgrade to its flagship AI model with better coding, sharper vision and a new ability to double-check its own work. Claude Opus 4.7 is their most capable generally available model, with a step-change improvement in agentic coding over Claude Opus 4.6.

## Claude Code CLI Availability

Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster by executing routine tasks, explaining complex code, and handling git workflows - all through natural language commands. Claude Opus 4.7 xhigh is now available for Claude Code, with an xhigh effort level for Opus 4.7 sitting between high and max, which can be tuned via /effort command to adjust speed vs. intelligence.

## Platform Availability

The API model ID is claude-opus-4-7, and it is available on all platforms: Anthropic API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry. Additionally, Claude Opus 4.7 is now rolling out on GitHub Copilot.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Based on the search results, **Opus 4.7 is available for Claude Code** — it launched April 16 with the model ID `claude-opus-4-7`. The search mentions an `xhigh` effort level specific to Opus 4.7, tunable via `/effort`.

You're likely on an older Claude Code version. Try:

```
claude update
```

If you're already on the latest version, it's possible the rollout is staged. You could also check:

```
claude --version
```

And compare against the [latest releases on GitHub](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases).

Sources:
- [Claude Opus 4.7 is generally available - GitHub Changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-04-16-claude-opus-4-7-is-generally-available/)
- [What's new in Claude Opus 4.7 - Claude API Docs](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/whats-new-claude-4-7)
- [Claude Code Releases - GitHub](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases)
- [Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7 - VentureBeat](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-releases-claude-opus-4-7-narrowly-retaking-lead-for-most-powerful-generally-available-llm)
- [Claude Opus 4.7 Complete Guide](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/925/claude-opus-4-7-complete-guide-features)

input.description: Check if claude is a symlink
Check file type of claude binary

content.text:
You're on **v2.1.98**, installed via the **standalone binary** method (not npm, not brew). It's a native arm64 binary at:

```
~/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.98
~/.local/bin/claude → (symlink to above)
```

Latest on GitHub is **v2.1.114**. To update this installation method, try:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | sh
```

That should download the new version and update the symlink. If that also fails, you could check what error you got — run it and see if it's a network or permissions issue.

input.description: Check npm with correct package name

content.text:
npm has **2.1.114**. The install script is serving you the stale 2.1.98 binary. You can install via npm instead:

```bash
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

This will put it in your npm global bin. You may need to ensure that takes precedence over `~/.local/bin/claude` in your PATH, or remove the old symlink after:

```bash
rm ~/.local/bin/claude
```
